title: Iconic SG landmarks (MBS / Helix / Esplanade / Supertrees / Flyer / Merlion)
desc: User reference (Pinterest 3D Singapore diorama) shows the 6 iconic landmarks must be procedurally modeled in correct silhouette. Create src/world/landmarks-sg.js exporting buildSGLandmarks(scene, proj). Each landmark per research/agent adec241: MBS 3 towers (194m, twin-leg base, boat skypark), Helix Bridge (280m TubeGeometry double helix), Esplanade twin durian (SphereGeometry hemispheres + InstancedMesh spike cones), Supertree Grove (18 trees with tapered trunk + canopy disc + 8 ribs), Singapore Flyer (TorusGeometry rim 150m + spokes + capsules), Merlion (LatheGeometry fish body + sphere head + water cylinder). Total ~25k tris budget. Place at real lat/lng (MBS 1.2834,103.8607; Helix 1.2877,103.8607; Esplanade 1.2897,103.8559; Supertrees 1.2820,103.8636; Flyer 1.2893,103.8634; Merlion 1.2868,103.8545). Wire into main.js after buildLandmarks.
verify: Drive into Marina Bay area via minimap; see boat-shaped MBS skypark on 3 towers + ferris wheel + supertree grove; landmarks dominate skyline.
commit: T16 iconic SG landmarks (MBS Helix Esplanade Supertrees Flyer Merlion)

title: Per-instance HSL variation
desc: User feedback: things look copy-paste fake. Research/agent a4762549 showed instanceColor HSL jitter (hue ±10deg sat ±20% lum ±15%) is the highest ROI fix. Apply to: palms.js (leaf and trunk colors per palm), buildings.js HDB body (already has but verify range), shophouse columns/AC, street lamps. Use deterministic hash by instance index so layout is stable across sessions. No shader changes — pure instanceColor BufferAttribute.
verify: Palms have varied green hues (yellow-green to teal-green); shophouses obviously different colors per house (already partial — extend); lamp poles slightly different gray tints.
commit: T17 per-instance HSL color jitter (palms + shophouses + lamps)

title: NPC traffic
desc: Sense-of-life upgrade. 40 NPC cars driving along OSM primary/trunk/motorway ways via CatmullRomCurve3. Per research/agent a80dd593: car has progress 0..1, advances by speed*dt/totalLen, getPointAt+getTangentAt drive matrix update. 3 InstancedMesh batches: sedan (Box body + Box wheels), bus (long box, yellow/red), taxi (sedan + 'taxi' top sign). When progress>=1 reassign to new way (simple random pick from filtered candidates). Spawn within 300m of player; far ones get zero-scale matrix. Body color per-instance via instanceColor jitter for sedans only. NO Rapier collider — pure visual; player drives through with no impact. setUsage(THREE.DynamicDrawUsage) on instanceMatrix. New file src/world/traffic.js. Wire into main.js per-frame tick.
verify: Driving any major road sees 5-10 cars moving along same/adjacent roads; at distance hidden via zero-scale; 60fps maintained.
commit: T18 NPC traffic (40 cars on OSM via CatmullRomCurve3)

title: Wind sway on palms
desc: Research/agent a4762549 onBeforeCompile vertex shader patch: per-instance phase via gl_InstanceID, vertex Y-weighted XZ jitter using uTime. Cheap mild gain. Patch leafMat in palms.js (only leaves sway, not trunk). Also patch customDepthMaterial so shadows match. Time uniform updated each frame from main.js tick. Watch for shader-cache invalidation — keep onBeforeCompile signature stable across reloads.
verify: Palm leaves visibly sway when stationary; no shadow flicker.
commit: T19 palm leaf wind sway via onBeforeCompile + gl_InstanceID

title: UI overhaul — SG design system
desc: User feedback: current UI is rubbish, no design language, dev-tool style breaks immersion. Built SSOT design tokens in src/ui/theme.js (SG red #ED2939 / gold #F5B344 / teal #3BC9DB on dark glass blur). Rewrote splash (cinematic SG title + flag accent + tri-color progress), speedometer (180° SVG arc gauge + animated needle + 10-segment boost), minimap (glass card + N/E/S/W compass + pulsing landmark dots), stats (hidden by default, backtick toggle, pill style), modal (glass card + landmark color rail + eyebrow tag). New src/ui/hud.js (auto-hiding controls pill, fades 6s), src/ui/info-bar.js (top-right time-of-day + landmark counter X/N), src/ui/district-banner.js (bottom-center entry banner reading districts.js SSOT). Cleaned index.html — removed legacy .hud div + clunky overlay panel.
verify: Live test via Claude Preview: splash shows SG flag + SINGAPORE + tri-color bar; HUD pill auto-fades; speedo arc needle animates; minimap has compass; info-bar shows DAY + 0/N; stats hidden until backtick toggle; no console errors.
commit: 962d936 UI overhaul — Singapore-themed driving game design system

title: Road network — fix stripes/junctions/curbs
desc: Audit found: (a) road-emitter walkAtSpacing maintains GLOBAL k across all ways, so dash phase carries between disconnected ways causing visible misalignment at junctions; (b) center stripe + edge stripe both at same Y with conflicting polygonOffset risk Z-fight; (c) sidewalks at tier.y+0.10 too thin to read as curb from cam height 5.5m; (d) road segment gaps where adjacent ways don't share endpoint vertex due to float precision in lat/lng projection. Fix: reset dash phase k=0 per-way in walkAtSpacing (or pass per-way mode), unify polygonOffset to single -1.5 for all paint, raise sidewalk to tier.y+0.18 and add explicit curb strip 0.12m tall, snap way endpoints to 0.05-unit grid before emitting geometry to close gaps.
verify: Live test: drive on motorway/primary — dashes are continuous along centerline (no phase jumps at way joins); curb edge clearly visible from car cam; no Z-fight flicker on stripes when looking down.

title: Building placement — fix yaw, water clip, district bleed
desc: Audit found: (a) shophouse yaw uses Math.atan2(perpZ,perpX) → buildings face PERPENDICULAR to street (long axis across road, looks alien); should use tangent (tanX,tanZ); (b) landmarks-sg.js and buildings.js place at Y=0 and any X/Z without checking water bbox or road centerline distance, so Esplanade dome clips into grass next to road and MBS could overlap CBD towers; (c) shophouse district filter uses w.g.some(inRegion) which includes ways that merely cross the bbox boundary, leaking shophouses outside chinatown. Fix: emit tanX/tanZ from walkAtSpacing alongside perpX/perpZ; rotate shophouses by atan2(tanX,tanZ); add minRoadClearance check (4m) — skip building if too close to any road centerline; switch district filter to MIDPOINT of each segment (not 'some vertex'); add landmark footprint AABB list, skip tower placement that overlaps any landmark AABB+10m buffer.
verify: Live test: shophouses on Chinatown roads have long axis PARALLEL to the street; no buildings sit on roads; MBS/Helix/Esplanade not overlapped by procedural towers; no shophouses bleeding into Marina Bay.

title: NPC traffic — fix teleport, height, lane offset
desc: Audit found: (a) when car progress>=1, traffic.js picks RANDOM new path from candidates → car teleports from old end to new start (potentially km away); (b) all cars hardcoded Y=0.4 regardless of road tier height (motorway tier.y=0.04 vs primary tier.y=0.02 → mismatch); (c) tan from getTangentAt not defensively normalized — near-zero derivatives at curve endpoints cause yaw noise; (d) cars drive on road CENTERLINE not in lane → NPC sedans appear to crash through each other on opposite directions on same road. Fix: on progress wrap, search candidates for path whose start point is within 30m of current car position (nearest neighbor); fall back to nearest-overall if none. Set car Y from road tier table (look up tier of source way). Normalize tan defensively (if length<0.001, reuse last tan). Offset cars by ±lane_offset (~1.4m) on perpendicular to tangent based on direction so left-side and right-side cars stay in correct lanes.
verify: Live test: drive alongside NPC traffic — cars stay in right-side lane (or left for opposite direction), no teleports when paths complete, Y-height matches road surface (no floating/sinking), no jitter on yaw at curve endpoints.

title: NPC traffic — Y-height per road tier
desc: T23 still hardcoded NPC car Y to 0.4m, but road tiers have varying surface Y (motorway 0.20, trunk 0.16, primary 0.12) so cars on lower tiers floated 0.2-0.3m above pavement. Fix: store TIER_Y table mirroring roads-osm.js, attach surfaceY per candidate, set car body Y = surfaceY + RIDE_HEIGHT (0.10). Cars now sit on the actual road surface regardless of tier.
verify: Live test (preview): NPC sedans visible at Helix/Merlion area sit on pavement; no floating. Switching tiers via path handover keeps Y consistent.

title: Road tier SSOT — extract to road-tiers.js
desc: T24 introduced TIER_Y as a copy in traffic.js; this guarantees drift. Created src/world/road-tiers.js as the single source of truth for tier table {t,w,color,y}. Exposes TIERS (frozen array), TIER_Y/TIER_W (lookup maps), surfaceY(tier). roads-osm.js and traffic.js now both import from it; no inline duplicates remain. Future road-aware subsystems (parked cars, awnings, drains) read from the same module.
verify: Live reload — no console errors; [traffic] 40 NPC cars; cars still ride at correct tier Y.
